observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is an older base snapshot than committed additive queue-update records.
The available GitHub connector can fetch known paths and recent commits but did not expose an exhaustive recursive tree endpoint in this run.
A local git clone attempt failed because the runtime could not resolve github.com.
inferred: Search results and recent commit lists cannot prove absence of undiscovered queue-update files.
Effective queue replay must remain fail-closed until its input manifest is derived from a non-truncated repository tree.
proposed: Feed a complete recursive Git tree response into tools/continuity/queue-discovery-manifest.mjs, then fetch and verify every recorded blob before replay.
superseded: Treating code search, recent commits, or known filenames as a complete queue-update inventory.
unresolved: No exhaustive tree response was obtainable through the connected tools, so the full queue corpus and effective state remain unproven.

durable_memory.statement: Discovery completeness is a property of the repository traversal, not of the filtering or replay code.
durable_memory.operational_rule: A manifest may set complete=true only when the upstream recursive tree explicitly reports truncated=false; downstream replay must reject every other manifest.

coverage: queue-update JSON filtering and deterministic ordering

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, blob identifiers, abstract operational rules, and test results only; no private chat, health, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads included.
